sourceMovie.title: Resident Evil: Apocalypse
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Resident Evil: Apocalypse for the same reason it broke through in 2004: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Alexander Witt keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Extinction
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Resident Evil: Apocalypse clicked for you, Extinction (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Alita: Battle Angel
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Resident Evil: Apocalypse clicked for you, Alita: Battle Angel (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Resident Evil: Apocalypse?
faq.answer: Try Extinction, Alita: Battle Angel, Rampage—each shares DNA with Resident Evil: Apocalypse while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Resident Evil: Apocalypse?
faq.answer: Resident Evil: Apocalypse (2004) is best described as Horror, Action, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Resident Evil: Apocalypse part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Resident Evil Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
